paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known significant developmental/intellectual disability and neurodevelopmental regression associated with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Parent reports] He just suddenly lost all muscle tone, smashed his face on the table, and now he won't stop having these stiffening seizures.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Acute seizure cluster with facial injury in a child with known Lennox-Gastaut syndrome.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden drop attack 30 minutes ago, followed by 3 tonic seizures
symptom_narrative.character: sudden loss of tone (drop attack) followed by repeated stiffening (tonic) seizures lasting 2-3 minutes each
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: not applicable — parent-reported, child non-verbal at baseline
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: repeated seizures without full return to baseline in between
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: increased hyperactivity and behavioural changes noted before the event, facial bleeding from a cut over the eyebrow after the fall
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known Lennox-Gastaut syndrome with multiple seizure types and developmental regression, on regular valproate, lamotrigine, and clobazam; missed this morning's clobazam dose
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: post-ictal, lethargic, bleeding from a facial cut, not returning to his usual baseline between seizures

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: regular daily doses, though this morning's clobazam dose was missed

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: parents present, appropriately padded the environment for drop attacks, protective helmet not worn at the time of the incident, some carer fatigue/burden noted

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: parents frightened by the combination of the seizure cluster and the facial injury, exhausted from ongoing care demands

access_barriers.transport: parents present but child requires ambulance given ongoing seizure risk and facial injury
